methods.description: A seismic survey was conducted to map the lake bed and sediments, followed by coring to collect sediment samples for analysis.

steps.description: A seismic survey of the lake bed and its underlying sediments was carried out using a Boomer system, coupled to a high-precision GPS.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct seismic survey using Boomer system and GPS

steps.description: Fifty-three transect lines were made across the lake with an interval spacing of 30 m, east-west and north-south.
steps.searchable_summary: create transect lines across lake for mapping

steps.description: Coring took place at 21.5 m water depth from a platform using a UWITEC stationary piston corer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform coring using UWITEC stationary piston corer

steps.description: The NAR10 cores were opened, split in two lengthwise, described and photographed in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: process and document sediment cores in laboratory

steps.description: The three core sequences were correlated visually at tie-points and best sections from three parallel cores were spliced together to create a Master Sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate and splice core sequences to create Master Sequence

methods.description: Analysis of stable isotopes and carbonate mineralogy to understand sediment composition and dating.

steps.description: Different mineralogies of CaCO3 were measured by X-ray diffraction.
steps.searchable_summary: measure CaCO3 mineralogies using X-ray diffraction

steps.description: Samples containing just calcite and/or aragonite were analysed for d18Ocarb and d13Ccarb via a VG Optima mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze isotopes using VG Optima mass spectrometer

steps.description: Organic fractions were disaggregated in HCl for 24 h and then washed with deionized water.
steps.searchable_summary: disaggregate organic fractions in HCl and wash

steps.description: Dried samples were homogenized and analysed for C/N and d13Corg on a Carlo Erba NA1500 and a VG Optima mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze C/N and d13Corg using Carlo Erba NA1500 and VG Optima

methods.description: Uranium-thorium dating to establish ages of sediment layers.

steps.description: Samples were analysed using a total dissolution isochron approach.
steps.searchable_summary: perform U-Th dating using total dissolution isochron approach

methods.description: Analysis of diatom samples to understand ecological changes over time.

steps.description: Sub-samples for diatom analysis were obtained by cutting sediments from core half sections using a scalpel.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain diatom sub-samples from core sections

steps.description: The diatom preparation procedure follows standard methods adapted from Battarbee et al. (2001).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare diatom samples using standard methods

steps.description: 300-400 diatom valves counted in transects on each slide.
Multidimensional scaling was performed on percentage data to identify major axes of data variability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform multidimensional scaling on diatom data

steps.description: Significant zone boundaries were identified using a constrained incremental sum-of-squares cluster analysis (CONISS) and the broken-stick approach.
steps.searchable_summary: identify zone boundaries using CONISS and broken-stick

steps.description: Transfer function training sets provided by the European Diatom Database (EDDI) were applied to the NAR10 diatom data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply transfer function training sets to diatom data
